query: phase 3 mcp resources impact analysis semantic search

results.title: Extended Minder Phase 2.x with retrieval substrate and orchestration adapter
results.content: Continued Phase 2.x by adding a semantic retrieval baseline (embedding providers, document store, vector store, RetrieverNode vector-first fallback-to-lexical behavior, and shared search substrate for minder_search_code) plus optional orchestration adapter layering (InternalGraphExecutor, LangGraphExecutorAdapter, orchestration runtime metadata, and runtime detection tests). Verified with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run ruff check ., uv run mypy src, and uv run pytest (76 passed).

results.title: Minder project architecture plan
results.content: Minder is a Full Agentic RAG MCP Server built with Python + LangGraph + Milvus + NV-Embed-v2 + Nemotron-3 Nano-4B GGUF. Architecture: MCP Transport Layer → LangGraph Orchestrator (Planning, Retriever, Reranker, Reasoning, LLM, Guard, Verification, Evaluator nodes) → Multi-tier Data Layer (Vector/Milvus, Relational/SQLite, Document/JSONL, Graph, Cache). 8 memory stores: Skill, History, Error, Session, Metadata, Document, Rule, Feedback. 5 implementation phases: Foundation → Agentic Pipeline → Advanced Retrieval + Knowledge Graph → Scale + Production → Self-Improvement. Plan stored at docs/PLAN.md.

results.title: Wired repo ingestion into Minder retrieval
results.content: Continued Phase 2.2 by adding a lightweight repo ingestion pipeline: DocumentStore upsert/get/delete helpers, IngestTools for file and directory ingestion, and automatic repo sync before minder_query/minder_search_code so vector-backed retrieval has real corpus data from the workspace. Added ingest tests and updated phase2x integration coverage. Verified with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run ruff check ., uv run mypy src, and uv run pytest (82 passed).

results.title: Minder switched to Qwen plan split
results.content: Updated the Minder planning docs to use Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B quantized GGUF for embeddings and Qwen3.5-0.8B quantized GGUF for the local LLM, both intended to run through llama.cpp via llama-cpp-python. Split docs/PLAN.md into an index plus docs/plan/01-06 focused documents covering scope, architecture, data/tools, workflow governance, implementation phases, and operations/delivery.

results.title: Docs refactor: split progress trackers by phase
results.content: Refactored monolithic docs/TASK_BREAKDOWN.md and docs/PROJECT_PROGRESS.md into an index + overview model. Added docs/progress/ with phase- and phase-part-specific markdown tables, each row linking to related requirements, design, architecture, or test docs to keep task context clean. Root docs now route readers into the relevant phase tracker instead of carrying the full backlog inline.
